title: JinPingMeiRecensionRecord
description: One evaluation item for the Jin Ping Mei recension-attribution / anachronistic-commentary benchmark.

chapter.description: Chapter number in the 100-chapter novel (same numbering across recensions).

recension.description: Textual base of the passage: cihua (1610, Wanli-era) or xiuxiang (Chongzhen-era, illustrated).

source_edition.description: Bibliographic pointer only -- do NOT store full copyrighted text here.

citation.description: Full citation of the edition/translation this excerpt is drawn from.

passage_excerpt.description: SHORT excerpt (<15 words) or a paraphrased summary of passage content. Full passages must be sourced by the annotator from a licensed copy and are NOT distributed with this dataset.

commentary_recension.description: Which recension this commentary tradition was actually written for.

note_summary.description: Paraphrased summary of the commentary's interpretive claim, not verbatim text.

task_type.description: recension_attribution: identify which recension a passage is from. anachronism_detection: identify whether a given interpretive frame is being misapplied across recensions. commentary_provenance: match a commentary note to its correct source recension.

prompt_template.description: How this item should be phrased to a model under evaluation, with placeholders like {passage} and {commentary_note}.

gold_label.description: Correct answer: a recension name, 'anachronistic'/'consistent', or a commentator name, depending on task_type.

rationale.description: Scholarly justification for the gold label, e.g. citing textual variants or known commentary provenance.

difficulty.description: easy: recension-marker vocabulary differs plainly. hard: requires tracking an interpretive claim across a translation that merges recensions (the Roy/Zhang Zhupo case).

annotator.description: Who created/verified this item -- for inter-annotator agreement tracking.

notes.description: Free-text annotator notes, e.g. ambiguity flags.
